commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d0129adabca456d808b986e36f08ea6cd68f0419)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes embedded compaction routing to carry Codex/OpenAI runtime selection through direct and queued fallback compaction, adds regression coverage, fixes several type-test drifts, and removes tokenjuice from the root shrinkwrap.

PR surface: Source +58, Tests +187, Other -17. Total +228 across 16 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, not from a live run: current main hardcodes the compaction target runtime to openclaw, and the linked issue gives a concrete Codex OAuth compaction failure path without OPENAI_API_KEY.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer or contributor needs to repair the routing guard, resolve the dirty merge state, and supply live Codex OAuth proof; automation cannot provide the missing contributor environment proof.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve Codex auth for compaction fallback
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86820.

OpenAI compaction fallback now keeps the selected Codex runtime route when Codex runtime metadata is available, while keeping the configured provider identity for context-window and config lookup. This lets Codex OAuth sessions route compaction model loading through `openai-codex` instead of falling back to direct `openai` API-key auth.

This also keeps direct OpenAI API-key compaction unchanged when no Codex runtime provider is configured, and leaves custom OpenAI-compatible base URLs untouched.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-runtime-context.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

`pnpm check:changed` delegated to Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01kstn684tqa5zy6t2z03yv2wt`, but the delegated run stopped producing output after setup and was cancelled. The earlier delegated run `tbx_01kstmz93ndp9reg6ecw9y6508` caught two type errors; both were fixed and the local core typecheck above passed afterward.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex OAuth-backed OpenAI compaction fallback no longer drops the Codex runtime route and asks for direct `OPENAI_API_KEY` when `openai-codex` runtime provider metadata is present.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout with focused regression tests and core typecheck; no live Codex OAuth account flow was executed from this checkout.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-runtime-context.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`; `git diff --check`; autoreview command above.

Evidence after fix: Regression tests cover Codex runtime OpenAI compaction without explicit Codex auth order, direct OpenAI API-key preservation when no Codex runtime provider is configured, OpenAI compaction model overrides through Codex runtime auth, custom OpenAI-compatible base URL preservation, and active direct compaction model loading through `openai-codex`.

Observed result after fix: Focused Vitest passed 5 files and 157 tests; core typecheck exited cleanly; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

What was not tested: A live installed OpenClaw gateway with real Codex OAuth compaction and no `OPENAI_API_KEY`; delegated `pnpm check:changed` hung after setup and was cancelled as noted above.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
